name: Rugalah/Elaine
recipeInstructions:
FILLING: Combine walnuts, raisins, sugar and cinnamon in a small bowl and blend well. Set aside. This is very forgiving. You can use jelly and different nut and flavor combinations. What ever tastes right to you and makes a nice thick filling.
DOUGH:
1. Combine the flour with sugar in a large bowl. Add the butter, eggs, honey, baking powder, and mix. You want a dough that mixes easily, not too stiff. You might need to add more flour to make it pliable enough.
2. Divide the dough into thirds. Flatten each into thin discs between sheets of waxed paper. Refrigerate at least 2 hours. THIS IS AN IMPORTANT STEP. The dough MUST rest and be COLD to work and handle properly.
3. Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Prepare your first cookie sheet by greasing and then flouring it and be sure to shake off the excess flour.
4. Remove one batch of dough from the refrigerator. Roll it out into a 12-inch circle, brushing generously with melted butter.
5. Sprinkle with about 1/3 of the filling, patting the mixture into the dough. Drizzle a little more butter over the top.
6. Cut into 16 wedges using a pizza cutter or very sharp knife. Roll each triangle up from the outside larger edge to the tiny point; curve slightly.
7. Arrange on prepared baking sheets point side down. Bake until golden, about 15 to 18 minutes, watching carefully to prevent burning. Repeat with remaining dough.
Note: This is not an easy dough to work with. You need everything COLD. If you have a pastry marble, this is the time to use it. I usually roll the dough right on my Formica counter.
